NCT04994509.name: PURPOSE-1
NCT04994509.group: Cisgender women and adolescent girls, sub-Saharan Africa, lenacapavir 927 mg SC Q6M vs daily oral F/TAF or F/TDF

allOutcomes.title: Incident HIV infection per 100 person-years (primary, vs background-HIV incidence)
Incident HIV, lenacapavir vs pooled F/TAF+F/TDF

NCT04994509.snippet: Source: Bekker LG et al. N Engl J Med 2024;391:1179-1192 (PURPOSE-1).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 5,338 cisgender women and adolescent girls aged 16-25 years from South Africa and Uganda randomized 2:2:1 double-blind double-dummy to lenacapavir 927 mg SC every 26 weeks (n=2,134), emtricitabine-tenofovir alafenamide (F/TAF, Descovy) daily oral (n=2,136), or emtricitabine-tenofovir disoproxil fumarate (F/TDF, Truvada) daily oral (n=1,068) as an active control with a background-HIV-incidence reference.

evidence.label: Primary - Zero Incident HIV Infections in Lenacapavir Arm
evidence.text: Over the interim analysis with median follow-up 9.1 months and 1,712 person-years of lenacapavir exposure: 0 incident HIV infections in lenacapavir arm vs 39 in F/TAF + F/TDF arms (pooled). Lenacapavir HIV incidence 0.00 per 100 person-years (95% CI 0.00 to 0.10); background HIV incidence 2.41 per 100 person-years. Relative risk <0.01 vs background (P<0.001). DSMB stopped trial early for overwhelming efficacy.

evidence.label: Secondary - Adherence and Safety
evidence.text: Injection-visit adherence in lenacapavir arm: 92% of scheduled Q6M injections received within window. No resistance-associated mutations emerged in the 0-infection arm. Injection-site reactions in 69% of lenacapavir arm (most mild-moderate). Overall safety comparable across arms.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization. D2 SOME CONCERNS - double-blind double-dummy IM+oral regimen is complex and interim-analysis unblinding was early (DSMB-triggered for overwhelming efficacy). D3 LOW - 95% primary follow-up through interim. D4 LOW - central HIV RNA + antibody testing. D5 LOW - SAP pre-specified, interim boundary pre-specified.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: FEMALE; min age 16 Years; max age 25 Years; healthy volunteers: t. NCT04925752.name: PURPOSE-2
NCT04925752.group: Cisgender men, transgender women, transgender men, non-binary people who have sex with men; lenacapavir SC Q6M vs daily oral F/TDF (Truvada)
NCT04925752.snippet: Source: Kelley CF et al. N Engl J Med 2024;391:1193-1204 (PURPOSE-2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 3,273 cisgender men, transgender women, transgender men, and gender non-binary people aged 16+ years who have sex with men randomized 2:1 double-blind double-dummy to lenacapavir 927 mg SC every 26 weeks (n=2,180) or F/TDF (Truvada) daily oral (n=1,087). Enrolled across 88 sites in 7 countries (USA, Argentina, Brazil, Mexico, Peru, Thailand, South Africa).

evidence.label: Primary - Incident HIV Infection
evidence.text: Over median follow-up 1.45 years: 2 incident HIV infections in lenacapavir arm vs 9 in F/TDF arm. Lenacapavir HIV incidence 0.10 per 100 person-years vs F/TDF 0.93 per 100 person-years. Rate ratio 0.11 (95% CI 0.03 to 0.38; P<0.001). 89% relative reduction.

evidence.label: Secondary - Superiority vs Background Incidence
evidence.text: Background HIV incidence estimated 2.37 per 100 person-years in a pre-specified screening cohort. Lenacapavir vs background: 96% relative reduction (P<0.001). Adherence to injection schedule 95%. No resistance in the 2 incident lenacapavir infections.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization. D2 SOME CONCERNS - double-blind double-dummy design with early DSMB unblinding for efficacy. D3 LOW - 97% primary follow-up. D4 LOW - central HIV testing. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 16 Years; healthy volunteers: t. title: RapidMeta Infectious Disease | Lenacapavir Twice-Yearly Injection for HIV Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis v1.1
